lesson.title: Poisson Distribution
lesson.body: The Poisson distribution models the number of events occurring in a fixed interval when events occur independently at a constant average rate О». If X ~ Poisson(О»), then P(X=k) = e^{-О»}О»^k/k! for k = 0, 1, 2, .... A key property is E[X] = Var(X) = О». It approximates Binomial(n,p) when n is large and p is small.

rooms.question: What is the mean and variance of a Poisson(О») random variable?
rooms.explanation: A defining property of the Poisson distribution: E[X] = Var(X) = О». This can be verified by direct calculation from the PMF.

rooms.question: A call center receives 3 calls per minute on average. What is P(X = 0) in any given minute?
rooms.explanation: P(X=0) = e^{-О»}В·О»вҒ°/0! = e^{-3} вүҲ 0.0498. The probability of zero calls in a minute.

rooms.question: For X ~ Poisson(О»), what is the PMF at k = 2?
Order the conditions under which Binomial(n,p) вүҲ Poisson(О») from first to last:
rooms.explanation: First identify the regime (n large, p small), set О»=np, verify the rule of thumb npвүӨ5, then apply the Poisson formula.

rooms.question: Earthquakes occur at rate 2 per year. What is the probability of at least 1 earthquake in a year?

boss.name: The Count of Poisson
boss.taunt: I count the uncountable. Each event independent, each arrival merciless.

questions.question: If X ~ Poisson(2) and Y ~ Poisson(3) are independent, what is the distribution of X + Y?
questions.explanation: The sum of independent Poisson random variables is Poisson: X + Y ~ Poisson(О»вӮҒ + О»вӮӮ) = Poisson(5).

questions.question: What is the mode of Poisson(О») when О» is not an integer?
questions.explanation: When О» is not an integer, the mode is вҢҠО»вҢӢ (floor of О»). When О» is an integer, both О» and О»вҲ’1 are modes.

questions.question: A website gets 100 hits/hour. Visits are Poisson. What is the standard deviation of hits per hour?
questions.explanation: For Poisson(О»), Var(X) = О», so Пғ = вҲҡО» = вҲҡ100 = 10.
